item.title: As Global Oil Prices Skyrocket, US Crude Is Lagging Behind
item.description: Benchmark oil prices are soaring as war in the Middle East roils trading flows, but the rally is uneven across the globe, sparking some of the largest gaps between US crude and the rest of the world in years.

item.title: Micron Gives Upbeat Forecast After Demand Soars for Memory Chips
item.description: Micron Technology Inc., the largest US maker of computer memory chips, offered a positive forecast for the current quarter after surging prices for the components bolstered its prospects.

item.title: Criminal Charges Amp Up Growing State Resistance to Kalshi
item.description: The rapid expansion of prediction markets is facing increasingly aggressive pushback from state officials pursuing a sprawling patchwork of lawsuits and cease-and-desist orders.

item.title: Powell Says No Plans to Leave Fed Until Investigation Over
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said he plans to stay at the central bank until after the Justice Department’s investigation is complete.

item.title: Venezuela Oil Exports to US Double on Easing Trade Restrictions
item.description: US fuel makers are boosting purchases of crude from Venezuela to the highest in more than a year, making good on the Trump administration’s goal of taking more crude from the country as the Iran war reshuffles global oil flows.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Michele Says Fed Signal Is ‘Don’t Worry About It’
item.description: JPMorgan Investment Management’s Bob Michele said the Federal Reserve sent a “don’t worry about it” signal to markets spooked by surging oil prices and heightened geopolitical risk because of the war in Iran.

item.title: Iran War May Delay Deal Timelines, Not Derail M&A, McMaster Says
item.description: War with Iran may slow the pace of dealmaking but it’s unlikely to dent overall M&A activity, according to Lazard Inc.’s global head of mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Still Projects One Rate Cut in 2026
item.description: Federal Reserve officials left interest rates unchanged and continued to expect one rate cut this year as they acknowledged increased uncertainty due to war in the Middle East.

item.title: Key Takeaways From Fed Decision to Hold Rates Steady
item.description: Here are key takeaways from the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate decision on Wednesday:

item.title: UK Oil, Gas Lobby Group Urges Tax Reform to Reduce LNG Imports
item.description: Faster reform of the UK’s North Sea windfall tax could significantly cut reliance on LNG imports, the industry lobby group said, as war in the Middle East highlights energy supply vulnerabilities.

item.title: Tiny AI Drone Technology Company Surges 1,200% in Two Days
item.description: Artificial intelligence drone software maker Swarmer Inc.’s shares have surged more than 1,200% in two days, making it the best market entry for a US stock in nearly a year.

item.title: Polymarket Seeks Chief Risk Officer After Legal Hiring Spree
item.description: Prediction markets platform Polymarket is looking to hire a chief risk officer as it works to expand its regulated business in the US, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wheat Jumps as Iran War, Wintry Weather Stoke Inflation Concerns
item.description: Wheat was climbing as the war in Iran continued to push up oil prices, prompting worries that farmers could cut back on sowing due to soaring costs for fuel and fertilizer.

item.title: Analysis of Fed Rate Decision, Powell News Conference
item.description: Jerome Powell Photographer: Al Drago/Bloomberg

item.title: Spinoff Stocks Beat the S&P 500 While Conglomerate Shares Fall Behind
item.description: Pared down spinoffs are having a renaissance as the pace of their stock gains against multi-industry companies quickens. That’s led even the parent of the New York Rangers and Knicks to weigh a breakup.

item.title: EU Moves to Ban AI That Creates Nonconsensual Sexual Images
item.description: Artificial intelligence tools that can be used to create nonconsensual sexual images may be banned in the European Union, following outcry after users used Elon Musk’s Grok service to generate thousands of undressed pictures of women and children.

item.title: Google Ties Data Center to 20-Year Power Deal, Solar Investment
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is moving ahead with plans for a major data center in Michigan that features a 20-year electricity contract requiring it to cover the full expense of adding a haul of new clean power.

item.title: South Korean Rocket Maker Innospace Says Gas Leak Caused Failure
item.description: The failed launch of a South Korean rocket in Brazil last December was caused by a gas leak, according to its developer Innospace Co., which is gearing up for another attempt in the third quarter of this year.

item.title: Chinese Partnerships With European Universities Stir Security Concerns
item.description: Cybersecurity research is a key focus.

item.title: Brazil Treasury Extends Record Intervention as Volatility Surges
item.description: Brazil’s Treasury is stepping into markets for a third straight day, counting on a record intervention to contain volatility triggered by a surge in oil prices amid escalating tensions around the war in Iran.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Has Revived Half Its Oil Exports Via Hormuz Bypass
item.description: Saudi Arabia has already ramped up its oil exports to more than half of normal levels despite the disruptions from the Iran war, an early sign of success for the kingdom’s ambitious contingency plan to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Goldman Tells Dealmakers Not to Wait for Perfection in M&A Chase
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. bankers say companies can’t afford to wait out the latest wave of volatility gripping markets to pursue strategic mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: Russia Plans Naval Convoys for Merchant Ships, Putin Aide Says
item.description: Russia plans to provide naval convoys to protect merchant ships as it seeks to bolster the defense of the nation’s vessels and so-called shadow fleet.

item.title: Wall Street Looking to Offload Billions of Dollars of Debt Backing Buyouts
item.description: War raging. Oil over $100. Inflation poised to accelerate. Leveraged finance bankers have been here before.

item.title: Pimco Steers Clear of ‘Pretty Bad’ Private Credit Loans on Sale
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is staying away from loans being put up for sale amid the tumult in the $1.8 trillion private credit market because they’re “pretty bad,” according to its president Christian Stracke.

item.title: Traders Bet on Two ECB Hikes This Year as Energy Prices Jump
item.description: Traders are fully pricing two interest-rate hikes from the European Central Bank this year, as an attack on Iranian gas assets revives fears of an inflation spike.

item.title: Nigeria’s Hard-Won Inflation Gains Threatened by War in Iran
item.description: Nigeria, which only recently cooled inflation from 31.5%, is back on alert as the Iran war drives up gasoline and diesel prices.

item.title: Canada’s Population Falls for Second Consecutive Quarter
item.description: Canada’s population declined again in the fourth quarter as the number of non-permanent residents in the country continued to fall.

item.title: Bitcoin Retreats From Six-Week High as Iran Conflict Escalates
item.description: Bitcoin retreated from a six-week high after a broader risk-off move swept across global financial markets amid growing concern of an escalation in the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Giant IPOs From SpaceX to OpenAI Put Index Rules Under Pressure
item.description: For decades, the path into America’s most important stock benchmarks followed a predictable rhythm: go public, prove yourself, wait your turn. Now the three firms that control those benchmarks are considering whether to rewrite the rules to absorb trillion-dollar IPOs from SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic.
